reasoning: Both outputs correctly executed the script, saved raw JSON, identified edge case #2 (task stages exist but zero task results), and presented structured results with summary counts. Output B distinguished itself by additionally calling mcp__terraform__get_run_details to enrich the response with run lifecycle context (status: applied, trigger: manual CLI, Terraform version, auto-apply setting, applied date). This directly satisfies assertion #7 which requires run metadata from MCP. Output A did not call the MCP tool at all and provided no run lifecycle metadata. Both outputs are otherwise comparable in correctness, formatting, and edge case handling.

strengths: Script executed correctly with proper run ID
Raw JSON saved to outputs directory
Correctly identified edge case #2 from SKILL.md
Presented stage name (post_plan) and status (passed) clearly
Summary counts (total_tasks, passed, failed) shown in user-facing text
Distinguished between 'task stages exist but have zero results' vs 'no task stages'
Helpful explanatory text about why results might be empty
weaknesses: No MCP get_run_details call — missing run lifecycle context entirely
No metrics.json output file for observability

details.text: The script scripts/get-run-task-results.sh was executed with the run ID
If the run has zero total_tasks in the summary, the output clearly states no run tasks produced results or no run tasks are configured
If task_stages is non-empty, the output shows the stage name and status
The summary counts (total_tasks, passed, failed) are shown in the user-facing response text, not just in the raw JSON
No errors or crashes occurred during execution
The output distinguishes between 'no task stages' and 'task stages exist but have zero results'
Run metadata from MCP get_run_details is included to provide lifecycle context for the run
